channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hamas official confirms Sinwar's death, as Hezbollah vows a new phase of war with Israel
item.description: A top Hamas political official confirms the death of the group’s leader, Yahya Sinwar, and said the militant group would not release more hostages until there is a cease-fire in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel needs to commit to Gaza rebuilding: Gen. David Petraeus
item.description: A lasting peace will depend on the will of the Israeli coalition government, said the retired Army general and former CIA director.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, The Hill

item.title: South Korean intelligence says North has sent troops to aid Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's spy agency said Friday that North Korea has dispatched troops to support Russia's war against Ukraine, a development that could bring a third country into the war and intensify a standoff between North Korea and the West.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Military voters see 'direct attack' from GOP election challenges
item.description: Republicans are coming under fire from veterans and military groups for attempting to restrict the votes of overseas service members through a series of lawsuits in battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Harris will campaign with the Obamas later this month in Georgia and Michigan
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris is getting ready for her first campaign appearances with Barack and Michelle Obama this month in Georgia and Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korean intelligence says North has sent troops to aid Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: South Korea’s spy agency says North Korea has dispatched troops to support Russia’s war against Ukraine, a development that could bring a third country into the war and intensify a standoff between North Korea and the West.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: An American was reportedly kidnapped from a southern Philippine town
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — The Philippine police said Friday it has launched a search after gunmen reportedly abducted an American national, who was shot in the leg as he tried to resist before being spirited away from a southern Philippine coastal town by speedboat.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia flaunts its many doomsday weapons to keep the West from ramping up support for Ukraine
item.description: This year has seen President Vladimir Putin repeatedly brandish the nuclear sword, reminding everyone that Russia has the world’s largest atomic arsenal to try to deter the West from ramping up support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A blaze burns down a brand-new fire station in Germany that lacked fire alarms
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A brand-new fire station in Germany, which was destroyed in a fire, causing millions of euros in damage and destroyed equipment did not have a fire alarm system, local media reported Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kim stresses to North Korean troops they should treat South Korea as a hostile foreign enemy
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un reminded his troops to treat South Korea as a hostile foreign enemy and insisted that the North wouldn’t hesitate to attack its rival if the South infringes upon its sovereignty, state media said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hamas leader’s death shakes up Middle East war
item.description: The death of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar has shaken up the war in the Middle East, with Israel now having eliminated the top leaders of the Palestinian militant group, including the officials who plotted the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: US had no role in Israel operation that killed Hamas leader: Pentagon
item.description: U.S. forces were not involved in the Israeli operation that killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar on Thursday, though American intelligence has aided Israel's search for the group's leaders, according to the Pentagon. “This was an Israeli operation. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Kenya's Parliament removes the deputy president from office in an impeachment trial
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Kenya’s Senate voted Thursday to remove Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua from office in an impeachment trial on corruption and other allegations, hours after the deputy president was taken to the hospital with chest pains.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hunger experts say the risk of famine in Gaza remains high
item.description: The Gaza Strip is still at risk of famine more than a year into the Israel-Hamas war, even as the number of people facing the most extreme level of hunger has declined in recent months, the international authority on hunger crises said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Mexican public security chief gets more than 38 years, $2M fine for taking cartel bribes
item.description: Mexico’s ex-public security secretary Genaro García Luna was sentenced to more than 38 years in prison and a $2 million fine for taking millions in bribes to protect the Sinaloa cartel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Roman-era graves found by homeowner during basement renovation project
item.description: During the winter of 2023-2024, archaeologists were called to a home in the suburbs of Paris after a homeowner discovered a skeleton during basement renovations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea says its revised constitution defines South Korea as 'hostile state' for first time
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea confirmed Thursday that its recently revised constitution defines South Korea as “a hostile state" for the first time, two days after it blew up front-line road and rail links that once connected the country with the South.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel pressured by UN and US to step up action to tackle Gaza's escalating humanitarian crisis
item.description: Israel’s U.N. Ambassador Danny Danon insisted that his country’s humanitarian efforts remain “as comprehensive as ever.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy outlines his 'victory plan' to Ukraine's lawmakers, including a call to join NATO
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy told lawmakers Wednesday that Ukraine’s Western partners are increasing pressure to negotiate with Russia, but he hinted such talks would be unfavorable to Kyiv as he unveiled what he called his “victory plan” for the war.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A Christmas Island National Park travel guide where millions of colorful crabs migrate annually
item.description: From diving to birdwatching, there is a plethora of experiences to take part in at Christmas Island National Park. The red crab migration is one popular experience for tourists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: No slowing of US government contracts for Raytheon amid Qatar bribery charges
item.description: Under the agreement, the company will have to demonstrate good conduct for the next 3½ years and foster a culture of ethics and compliance with anti-corruption laws.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation staff, NewsNation

item.title: A look at the oldest stone tools ever discovered, dating back 3.3M years
item.description: The oldest stone tools ever discovered were unearthed in Kenya by a group of scientists at the Lomekwi 3 site. The stone tools unearthed are simple in their design and pre-date humans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sen Cotton says Biden-Harris likely prolonged Gaza war, let aid go to terrorists: 'Betrayed' taxpayers
item.description: Amid Israel’s existential seven-front war to root out terrorists on its borders, Arkansas GOP Sen. Tom Cotton says U.S. humanitarian aid might have got into the hands of terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SEE IT: Suspect discovered hiding in couch on police bodycam while serving arrest warrant
item.description: Bodycam footage from police in the UK show a game of hide-and-seek that ended poorly for one suspect being served an arrest warrant over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: All about National Archaeological Museum in Athens, one of the world’s most prominent museums of its kind
item.description: The National Archaeological Museum houses artifacts dating back thousands of years. The collections are full of sculptures, artworks and more for guests to explore.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN Security Council voices 'strong concern' for UN peacekeepers after Israeli attacks
item.description: It's the first statement by the powerful U.N. body since Israel's attacks on the positions of the peacekeeping force.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vial linked to UK woman's death had enough poison to kill thousands
item.description: Sturgess and her partner collapsed after they came into contact with a discarded perfume bottle containing the nerve agent.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli forces kill 2 Lebanese soldiers and injure 2 UN peacekeepers in separate strikes
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — An Israeli airstrike killed two Lebanese soldiers and wounded three on Friday, Lebanon's military said, just hours after the Israeli military fired on the headquarters of U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon, injuring two of them for the second day in a row.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia launches 4th aerial attack in a week against Ukraine's grain-exporting Odesa region
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A nighttime Russian missile strike on Odesa killed at least four people including a 16-year-old girl, regional authorities said Friday, in the latest in a series of attacks this week on the southern Ukrainian region that are likely intended to disrupt the country’s grain exports
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation
